title: Hieracium floccipedunculum
intro: Hieracium floccipedunculum, also known as woolly hawkweed, is a perennial plant species in the dandelion family (Asteraceae) characterized by its distinctive woolly leaves and yellow flowers. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, as well as potential antiviral and antioxidant activities. Bioactive constituents of Hieracium floccipedunculum include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are thought to contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Hieracium floccipedunculum is often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to treat various ailments, including digestive issues and skin conditions.
taxonomy: Hieracium floccipedunculum belongs to the family Asteraceae. It is a species of hawkweed, classified within the genus Hieracium. The genus Hieracium is further classified within the tribe Lactuceae. This species is native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. It is typically found growing in rocky or sandy areas at high elevations.
uses: Hieracium floccipedunculum is a species of hawkweed, but I couldn't find any information on its specific medicinal uses.
